subject: GRB 240824C: Fermi GBM Final Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

"At 15:22:15.95 UT on 24 August 2024, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM)
triggered and located GRB 240824C (trigger 746205740/240824640).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data,
is RA = 164.53, Dec = -28.50 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 10h 58m, -28d 30'),
with a statistical uncertainty of 5.97 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 36 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240824640/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn240824640.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240824640/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn240824640.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240824640/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn240824640.gif"
